texto: AttractionThe whites of his eyespull me like moons.He smiles. I believehis face. Alreadymy body slips down in the chair:I recline on my side,offering peeled grapes.I can taste his tonguein my mouthwhenever he speaks.I suspect he lies.But my body oils itself loose.When he gets up to fix a drinkmy legs like derrickshoist me off the seat.I am thirsty, it seams.Already I see the seductionfar off in the distancelike a large treedwarfed by a risein the road.I put away objectionsas quietly as quilts.Already I explain to myselfhow marriages are broken--accidentally, like arms or legs.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Enid Shomer: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de limites e escolhas conscientes. O trecho “AttractionThe whites of his eyespull me like moons.He smiles. I believehis face. Alreadymy body s…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que educacao, attraction, cheating pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de casamento e viagem.

texto: How could you determine a man's intention if you didn't speak his language or share his beliefs? She'd happily embarked on a study of ancient Egyptian religion but had no curiosity about Islam, which seemed an amalgam of oddities and borrowings. She felt with conviction what she'd written home more than once--that Egypt would be an exquisite country if not for the Egyptians who lived there.
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de Enid Shomer que provoca esforço, persistência e resultados. Ao questionar “How could you determine a man's intention if you didn't speak his language or share his beliefs? …”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre educacao, egypt, islam, com foco em estudos e islam.

texto: Ambition was a dull pain, like a continually broken heart.
